meta_title: For Students: idea validation to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Idea Validation to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Stop building in the dark. Learn how to use idea validation to overcome a lack of marketing skills with this beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: Marketing isn't a magical talent; it is a data-driven feedback loop. By using idea validation, you replace guesswork with empirical evidence. Use Antigravity to simulate market demand and Claude Code to iterate on your MVP landing page, ensuring you only build features that your target audience is actively demanding today.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks hunched over your terminal, crafting the perfect codebase, only to release your project into a deafening silence. The 'build it and they will come' myth is the graveyard of thousands of brilliant student projects. You aren't failing because your code is bad; you are failing because you are solving a problem that nobody is paying to fix.

But here is the shift: you don't need a marketing degree to win. You need an engineer's mindset. By treating your startup idea like a hypothesis, you can validate demand before you write a single line of production code. Let’s turn your technical curiosity into a market-validated machine.

steps.title: Define the Problem Hypothesis
steps.description: Before coding, articulate the specific pain point your user faces. If you cannot describe the problem in one sentence, you aren't ready to build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a market researcher. Generate 5 specific, high-friction problems for [Target Audience] in the [Industry] sector that are currently underserved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a markdown file named hypothesis.md with the top 3 problem statements derived from my research.'

steps.title: Automate Competitor Sentiment Analysis
steps.description: Understand what users hate about existing solutions. This is your 'marketing' edge: building what others ignore.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze common complaints on G2 or Reddit for [Competitor Name]. Identify 3 feature gaps that users are constantly requesting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Search for recent discussions on [Topic] and extract a summary of user frustrations into a insights.txt file.'

steps.title: Draft the Value Proposition
steps.description: Create a landing page copy that speaks directly to the pain point identified in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write 3 variations of a hero section headline for a landing page that focuses on the 'No Marketing Skills' problem, using the PAS (Problem-Agitation-Solution) framework.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update index.html with the highest-converting headline variations generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Simulate User Feedback Loops
steps.description: Use AI to roleplay as your target customer to stress-test your value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a skeptical potential user. Review my landing page copy and identify 3 reasons why you would not sign up for this service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Critique the current landing page content based on the persona of a skeptical [Role] and suggest improvements.'

steps.title: Execute the Concierge MVP
steps.description: Build the simplest possible interaction to capture leads. Don't build the app; build the waitlist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a simple React component for an email capture form with a compelling call-to-action that emphasizes early access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new component file for the waitlist form and integrate it into the main App.tsx.'

pro_tips: Never build a backend feature until at least 50 people have signed up for your waitlist.
Use the 'Mom Test' framework: ask if they have experienced the problem, not if they like your idea.
Your landing page is your product. If people don't click, your messaging is the bug, not the code.
Treat every 'no' as a data point that saves you 20 hours of wasted development time.
Focus on micro-niches; a small, obsessed audience is infinitely more valuable than a generic, indifferent one.
resources: The Mom Test Book Summary: https://momtestbook.com
content.next_steps: Once you have your waitlist live, start reaching out to 10 potential users manually on LinkedIn or Twitter to validate the problem through conversation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap to validate any idea without needing a marketing budget. Stop guessing and start building with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
